d4b_e1_team_crisis.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_team_crisis.text: Day 4, and your team is running on empty. Low morale has become visible to customers, and it's starting to affect everything.

d4b_e1_crisis_b.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_crisis_b.text: Boss, I need to be honest. Two team members are thinking of quitting. The pressure and low morale have become too much. We need something to change today.

choices.text: Have an immediate one-on-one with each person
Offer immediate pay increases to retain them
Restructure responsibilities to reduce pressure
Tell them you understand if they need to leave

d4b_e1_oneonone.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_oneonone.text: Just having you sit down and listen made a huge difference. I feel heard for the first time in weeks.

d4b_e1_pay.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_pay.text: The pay increase helps, but honestly, it's not just about money. We hope things change.

d4b_e1_restructure.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_restructure.text: Changing the structure actually makes a real difference. You addressed the specific pressure points. Thank you.

d4b_e1_leave.speaker: Staff
d4b_e1_leave.text: You actually mean that? One decided to stay. The other is putting in notice tomorrow.

d4b_e1_result_oneonone.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_oneonone.text: Direct personal attention is the most powerful retention tool. People leave managers, not companies.

d4b_e1_result_pay.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_pay.text: Compensation addresses symptoms but rarely the root cause of burnout. Use the time it buys to fix underlying issues.

d4b_e1_result_restructure.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_restructure.text: Addressing root causes rather than symptoms is exceptional leadership. Burnout is usually a systems problem.

d4b_e1_result_leave.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e1_result_leave.text: Graceful exits preserve dignity. The one who stayed chose to — that voluntary commitment is more valuable than reluctant presence.

d4b_e2_bad_review.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_bad_review.text: The internal struggles are now public. A customer left a detailed review describing staff as disengaged and the place as unwelcoming.

d4b_e2_review_b.speaker: You
d4b_e2_review_b.text: The review is spreading. It describes exactly what I feared — our internal struggles are now visible to customers. I need to act on both fronts.

choices.text: Respond to the review AND fix team issues simultaneously
Fix the team first — customers come second today
Address the public review first — protect the brand
Bring in a consultant to assess the situation

d4b_e2_both.speaker: Staff
d4b_e2_both.text: You're juggling a lot, but addressing us AND the public issue simultaneously shows real commitment.

d4b_e2_team.speaker: Staff
d4b_e2_team.text: Choosing us over the public image means everything. We'll work twice as hard knowing you prioritize your people.

d4b_e2_review.speaker: Customer Online
d4b_e2_review.text: I saw your professional response to my review. It was thoughtful. I hope things improve internally too.

d4b_e2_consult.speaker: Consultant
d4b_e2_consult.text: After reviewing the situation, the core issue is unclear role expectations and insufficient recognition. Both are fixable within a week.

d4b_e2_result_both.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_both.text: Managing internal and external crises simultaneously is exhausting but necessary. Great leaders don't get to choose which fire to fight.

d4b_e2_result_team.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_team.text: People-first leadership has short-term costs but builds loyalty that recovers reputations faster than any PR strategy.

d4b_e2_result_review.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_review.text: Brand protection buys time but isn't a solution. Use the goodwill you rebuild to create space for genuine internal change.

d4b_e2_result_consult.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e2_result_consult.text: External perspective on internal problems breaks blind spots. Asking for help is wisdom, not weakness.

d4b_e3_customer_complaint.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_customer_complaint.text: A frustrated customer is at the counter. Their laundry was damaged in one of your machines.

d4b_e3_complaint_b.speaker: Customer
d4b_e3_complaint_b.text: This is my favorite shirt, and your machine ruined it! What are you going to do about this?

choices.text: Apologize sincerely and offer full compensation
Offer a discount on future services only
Blame the machine and offer nothing

d4b_e3_compensate.speaker: Customer
d4b_e3_compensate.text: Thank you for making it right. I'll give you another chance.

d4b_e3_discount.speaker: Customer
d4b_e3_discount.text: A discount on future services? That doesn't help me replace my shirt today.

d4b_e3_blame.speaker: Customer
d4b_e3_blame.text: I'm never coming back here. And I'm telling everyone about this.

d4b_e3_result_compensate.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_result_compensate.text: Full ownership of mistakes builds trust that lasts. The cost hurts now but buys loyalty for years.

d4b_e3_result_discount.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_result_discount.text: Partial solutions for problems you caused rarely satisfy. Customers want the problem fixed, not a coupon.

d4b_e3_result_blame.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e3_result_blame.text: Deflecting blame when you're at fault destroys reputation completely. Accountability is always better than excuses.

d4b_r_staff_sick.speaker: Staff
d4b_r_staff_sick.text: Boss, two people called in sick today. We're already short-staffed. This is going to be rough.

d4b_r_sick_b.speaker: You
d4b_r_sick_b.text: A skeleton crew on an already tough day. How do you handle it?

choices.text: Work alongside the team to cover shifts
Close early today
Call in a temp worker

d4b_r_sick_work.speaker: Staff
d4b_r_sick_work.text: You working alongside us? That's huge. We'll get through this together.

d4b_r_sick_close.speaker: Customer
d4b_r_sick_close.text: Closed early again? This is the second time this week.

d4b_r_sick_temp.speaker: Staff
d4b_r_sick_temp.text: The temp is helping, but training them slows us down. Still, better than nothing.

d4b_r_sick_result_work.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_sick_result_work.text: Leading from the front during crises builds team respect. Your willingness to get your hands dirty sets the culture.

d4b_r_sick_result_close.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_sick_result_close.text: Closing early protects your team but frustrates customers. Communicate clearly when you have to do it.

d4b_r_sick_result_temp.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_sick_result_temp.text: Temporary solutions have temporary costs. They buy time but don't solve underlying staffing issues.

d4b_r_machine_vandalism.speaker: Staff
d4b_r_machine_vandalism.text: Boss! Someone spray-painted graffiti on the outside wall overnight. Customers are noticing.

d4b_r_vandal_b.speaker: You
d4b_r_vandal_b.text: Vandalism on top of everything else. How do you respond?

choices.text: Clean it immediately and file a police report
Leave it as a reminder of the area
Turn it into art with a mural

d4b_r_vandal_clean.speaker: Customer
d4b_r_vandal_clean.text: They cleaned it up fast. At least they care about how the place looks.

d4b_r_vandal_leave.speaker: Customer
d4b_r_vandal_leave.text: They're not even cleaning that graffiti? Doesn't say much about their standards.

d4b_r_vandal_mural.speaker: Customer
d4b_r_vandal_mural.text: They turned it into a beautiful mural! This place just keeps getting better.

d4b_r_vandal_result_clean.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_vandal_result_clean.text: Quick response to problems shows you care. Customers notice how you handle setbacks.

d4b_r_vandal_result_leave.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_vandal_result_leave.text: Leaving problems visible sends the wrong message. Your space reflects your standards.

d4b_r_vandal_result_mural.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_vandal_result_mural.text: Turning obstacles into opportunities is creative leadership. You transformed a problem into an asset.

d4b_r_utility_outage.speaker: Staff
d4b_r_utility_outage.text: Boss! The power just went out. No machines are running, and customers are waiting.

d4b_r_utility_b.speaker: You
d4b_r_utility_b.text: A power outage on an already difficult day. How do you handle the waiting customers?

choices.text: Apologize and offer refunds or vouchers
Ask customers to wait — power should return soon
Close temporarily until power returns

d4b_r_utility_refund.speaker: Customer
d4b_r_utility_refund.text: A refund and a voucher? That's generous. Power outages happen — you handled it well.

d4b_r_utility_wait.speaker: Customer
d4b_r_utility_wait.text: We've been waiting 20 minutes. Any update? This is frustrating.

d4b_r_utility_close.speaker: Customer
d4b_r_utility_close.text: Closed? I drove all the way here for nothing.

d4b_r_utility_result_refund.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_utility_result_refund.text: Generous service recovery during uncontrollable problems builds incredible loyalty. People remember how you treat them when things go wrong.

d4b_r_utility_result_wait.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_utility_result_wait.text: Asking people to wait without compensation or communication damages trust. Keep customers informed even when you can't fix the problem.

d4b_r_utility_result_close.speaker: Mentor
d4b_r_utility_result_close.text: Closing is sometimes necessary, but communicate clearly. A note on the door and social media post can save customer frustration.

d4b_e5_turning_point.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e5_turning_point.text: Despite everything, today was a turning point. End of day, and you have a chance to set a new standard — a reset moment for the whole team.

d4b_e5_reset_b.speaker: Staff
d4b_e5_reset_b.text: Things felt different today. Difficult, but different. Like we're finally being honest about what needs to change. What happens from here, boss?

choices.text: Announce a formal recovery plan with milestones
Declare today a reset — start fresh tomorrow
Ask the team what they need to move forward
Acknowledge mistakes and commit to doing better

d4b_e5_plan.speaker: Staff
d4b_e5_plan.text: A real plan with real milestones. Finally, something concrete to work toward.

d4b_e5_reset.speaker: Staff
d4b_e5_reset.text: A clean slate. Sometimes that's exactly what's needed. Thank you.

d4b_e5_ask.speaker: Staff
d4b_e5_ask.text: You're asking us? That alone changes everything. We have ideas we've been sitting on for weeks.

d4b_e5_acknowledge.speaker: Staff
d4b_e5_acknowledge.text: Hearing you acknowledge what went wrong means more than any bonus or plan.

d4b_e5_result_plan.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e5_result_plan.text: Recovery plans with clear milestones convert vague hope into trackable progress. Teams need to see the path, not just hear about the destination.

d4b_e5_result_reset.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e5_result_reset.text: Symbolic resets matter in team culture. The ritual of declaring a fresh start releases tension and creates permission to behave differently.

d4b_e5_result_ask.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e5_result_ask.text: The answers to most business problems already exist within your team. Leaders who ask unlock that wisdom.

d4b_e5_result_acknowledge.speaker: Mentor
d4b_e5_result_acknowledge.text: Accountability without defensiveness is rare leadership. When people see their leader own mistakes, it gives them permission to be honest too.
